prerequisites: A Flexport account with API access. Only account administrators can create API credentials.
An API key (access token) generated from the Flexport dashboard, provided in the connector's api_key field.
A start date (start_date) marking the earliest point from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: API Key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the single api_key secret field. Flexport attaches the credential to API requests as an HTTP header in the form 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'. Generate the credential from the Flexport dashboard before configuring the connector.

setupSteps.title: Open API Credentials in the Flexport dashboard
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Flexport as an account administrator. From the top navigation bar, go to your business Settings and open the API Credentials tab. Only account administrators can create API credentials.

setupSteps.title: Create credentials and select resources
setupSteps.body: Click Create Credentials, select and enable the resources you want to grant access to, then click Create. Flexport's public API uses OAuth 2.0; a Client ID and Client Secret are issued for the new credential, which are exchanged for an access token.

setupSteps.title: Obtain the access token
setupSteps.body: Exchange your credentials for an access token by sending a POST request to https://api.flexport.com/oauth/token with the client_id, client_secret, audience, and grant_type parameters, then read the access_token from the JSON response. The token is a JWT that expires after 24 hours, so cache and reuse it rather than requesting a new one per call.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the access token in the api_key field. Set start_date to the earliest date from which the connector should begin syncing records.
